title: API Error Envelope
description: Standard error response format for REST APIs. All error responses must conform to this schema.

code.description: Machine-readable error code. UPPER_SNAKE_CASE. Used by clients for programmatic error handling.

message.description: Human-readable error description. Safe to display to end users. Do not include internal details, stack traces, or database errors.

details.description: Optional array of granular error details. Used for validation errors with multiple fields or batch operations with partial failures.

field.description: Dot-notation path to the field that caused the error (e.g., 'address.zip_code').

reason.description: Human-readable explanation of why this specific field or item failed.

value.description: The rejected value, if safe to echo back. Omit for sensitive fields (passwords, tokens).

index.description: For batch operations: the zero-based index of the failed item in the request array.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this request. Use for cross-referencing with server-side logs and support tickets.

doc_url.description: Optional URL to documentation explaining this error and how to resolve it.

details.reason: Must be a valid email address.

error.message: The requested user does not exist.
Request limit exceeded. Retry after 45 seconds.
